title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot questions Pamela Hosford regarding the accuracy of her "Tragic Women" newspaper series. He notes discrepancies in her reports, such as incorrect trial dates, names, and locations. Hosford admits that the articles are a "hodgepodge of romantic stories" where she utilizes her imagination. Poirot questions her portrayal of specific women, including Eva Kane and Lily Gampel, noting that while some subjects moved abroad, they may have returned to the country. Following this, Poirot tells Spence that the supposedly "very good" nature of the people in Broadhurst might itself serve as a motive for murder.

Poirot then visits Crossroads Farm to meet Dr. Rendell. Posing as a defense lawyer seeking new evidence for an appeal in the Mrs. McKinty case, Poirot asks Dr. Rendell if Mrs. McKinty was an honest and truthful person, as she performed weekly cleaning services at his home. This line of questioning makes Dr. Rendell uneasy. During their conversation, Dr. Rendell discusses several local residents, including the Upwards and the Samuels, and mentions a young woman running a hotel. 

Dr. Rendell also shares an "inside story" regarding the Cottington case, claiming his uncle was the husband's doctor. He asserts that the woman involved in that case was not innocent, but instead manipulated a young man into committing murder to become a wealthy widow. Poirot reflects on how such sensationalized articles might provoke readers to investigate the current whereabouts of the women mentioned in the reports.
